programmes.show_description: Sleeping Partners: Robin Tripp, now a fully qualified, unemployed chef, harbours ambitions of opening his own bistro...for people who appreciate high prices.
The Bistro Kids: In the run-up to the bistro's launch, Robin must battle a stuffed bear, half a pig and a public health inspector. A tricky first customer causes calamity.
The Colonel's Horse: Col. Potter leaves for some R&R and asks Radar to look after his mare. Margaret asks Hawkeye to operate on her appendix.
Hawk's Nightmare: Havoc ensues when Radar removes a spirit post from the road. When a man is brought in for medical attention, he refuses surgery unless the camp spirits are exorcised.
Exorcism: Hawkeye is surprised to learn that he has been sleepwalking. All he knows is that he's been particularly depressed about the young age of the soldiers he is trying to keep alive.
Unforgettable Characters: Radar becomes an aspiring writer and begins writing his impressions of the camp. Hawkeye and BJ give Frank a surprise birthday present that only he will appreciate.
Rumpole and the Old Boy Net: Alongside his pupil Fiona Allways, Rumpole defends a couple charged with blackmail and with running an immoral house. But who are the couple trying to protect?
An uplifting round-up of good news stories from the UK and around the world.
Birds Of A Feather: Factory workers ignore ground-water contamination, even though it is making their children sick. Jonathan resorts to drastic measures to force them to face the facts.
An uplifting round-up of good news stories from the UK and around the world.
Popcorn, Peanuts, and Crackerjacks: Jonathan and Mark try to help a minor-league baseball team that can never seem to win.
An uplifting round-up of good news stories from the UK and around the world.

An uplifting round-up of good news stories from the UK and around the world.
Plane Death: Mark and Jonathan visit an old friend of Mark's but discover he has been missing for five days and encounter conspiracy of silence as they search for clues.
One Winged Angels: Jonathan has a tough time on an assignment when he falls in love with the woman he's supposed to be pairing up with another man.
The Monster of Walnut Grove: On the eve of Halloween, Mary and Laura hear a loud argument and, on inspection, witness what they believe to be the murder of Mrs Oleson.
Fred: To save him from farmer Parson's shotgun, Laura adopts a mischievous billy goat and names him Fred. But she finds he is more trouble than she bargained for.
The Bully Boys: Following Reverend Alden's lead, the Walnut Grove townsfolk try to turn the other cheek when the bad behaviour of the Galender brothers causes tempers to flare.
The Flight: Jim Bob befriends a boy who claims to be joining the Air Corps. Elizabeth adopts Maud as a grandmother, and Myrtle the goat has a baby.
The Milestone: Olivia reaches out to her aunt for help and advice, while Jim-Bob contemplates resorting to desperate measures to get a job.
A Few Dollars More: Dooley and Minto have a dangerous new business deal and arrange a supply of illicit liquor, but with the new Garda arriving in town, are they really away from prying eyes?
Apple Day: Ormston prepares for the traditional Apple Day Festival, while Len's cousin Nathan visits town.
...The Dear Children: Laura and Almanzo frantically search for their missing baby daughter, who they fear has been kidnapped. (1984)
Fallen Idol: Radar tells Hawkeye he thought going to war meant he would become a man but that nothing has happened to him. Things go horribly wrong when Hawkeye tries to cheer up his friend.
Last Laugh: Madness strikes as BJ and his old friend play a series of practical jokes on each other. Klinger leads an imaginary camel around, feeding and even cleaning up after it.
War of Nerves: The camp, caught up in tension and nerves, creates a bonfire to release their pressure. Sidney Freedman is depressed over a young soldier who blames him for his injuries.
The Winchester Tapes: Major Charles Winchester desperately wants to leave the 4077th and tape records a letter to his wealthy father begging him to get him transferred to the US.
The Light That Failed: The 4077th is running out of light bulbs. When the supply truck arrives, they find that they have received the wrong shipment, and in the middle of winter.
In Love and War: Hawkeye begins to fall in love with a Korean villager. Meanwhile, Margaret learns that her husband may have made a pass at a new nurse assigned to the 4077th.
Change Day: Major Winchester plots to cheat the natives when the Army changes the military scrip. Klinger tries to get into West Point so that he can get out of Korea.
Fallen Idol: Radar tells Hawkeye he thought going to war meant he would become a man but that nothing has happened to him. Things go horribly wrong when Hawkeye tries to cheer up his friend.
Last Laugh: Madness strikes as BJ and his old friend play a series of practical jokes on each other. Klinger leads an imaginary camel around, feeding and even cleaning up after it.
War of Nerves: The camp, caught up in tension and nerves, creates a bonfire to release their pressure. Sidney Freedman is depressed over a young soldier who blames him for his injuries.
The Fear Merchants: The Avengers' latest case sees them enter the world of ceramics when industry executives are mysteriously killed. Can Steed and Peel unearth the truth?
Crimewatch: Henry decides to join the neighbourhood watch after a spate of burglaries in the local area.
A Little Competition: Robin knew the restaurant next door would be stiff competition, but it turned out to be more than he bargained for. Vicky meets an old flame.
The Maternal Triangle: Robin is not keen to meet Vicky's mother, as he's heard she has a nasty habit of hitting people on the nose. The new dishwasher thinks he can cope single-handed.
An uplifting round-up of good news stories from the UK and around the world.
The Smile In The Third Row: Jonathan is sceptical of the lead actor in a Broadway play who claims that God is attending the show's performances.
An uplifting round-up of good news stories from the UK and around the world.
The Secret: The teenage daughter of Mark's friend learns she was adopted and seeks her biological mother, a quest that threatens to tear the family apart.
An uplifting round-up of good news stories from the UK and around the world.

An uplifting round-up of good news stories from the UK and around the world.
Going Home, Going Home: God grants Mark the chance to go back in time and tell his grandfather that he loved him.
An uplifting round-up of good news stories from the UK and around the world.
As Difficult As ABC: Brian Baldwin, a college basketball star, is diagnosed with cardiac insufficiency. After losing his scholarship he's forced to deal with his illiteracy.
The Hunters: Laura thinks she is old enough to go hunting with Pa. Charles lets her join him on a trip, which almost ends in disaster.
Blizzard: Miss Beadle dismisses her students early on Christmas Eve, unaware that the children will soon be caught in a howling blizzard.
The Celebration: After years of hard work, the Walton family are finally out of debt. Meanwhile, Corabeth tries to play matchmaker for the new preacher.
The Rumor: Elizabeth's vivid imagination threatens to get her in trouble when she starts a rumour about another family.
The Outsiders: Orla gives Conor the elbow while Frankie finds her own brand of justice for Dooley. Out in the field Liam and Donal are seeing things larger than life in a hedge.
The Milk of Human Kindness: Both Emma Rowe and Laura contract a fever after they are admitted to hospital, but it could be more than just a simple coincidence.
The Bramwells are celebrating Robert's forthcoming election to the Royal College of Physicians when his sister Emily reappears after running off with a married man 20 years earlier.
An uplifting round-up of good news stories from the UK and around the world.
Rumpole and the Sporting Life: Fiona persuades Rumpole to defend her sister Jennifer, who is accused of murdering her husband.
Images: Hawkeye and BJ try to discourage Radar from getting a tattoo. Hot Lips realises that there is a camaraderie of nurses at the 4077th and she is not included.
The M*A*S*H Olympics: Col Potter enforces an exercise course. When Klinger hears that being overweight can bring a discharge he decides to put on some extra pounds.
The Grim Reaper: Colonel Bloodworth antagonises Hawkeye, he retaliates by shoving him. Bloodworth presses for a court martial until he watches Hawkeye save a soldier's life.
Jacko is still being pursued by Lesley, but Veronica has a bust-up with Lionel and needs a shoulder. Jacko turns to Sandra for some solace.
Celia, the runaway nun Jacko helped, needs a place to stay. Jacko finds her proximity in the flat somewhat disconcerting and becomes a runaway himself.
Lionel's life becomes all mixed up when he loses his most precious possession. Jacko develops his outside interests and meets Linda, who brings a whole new ball game to his life.
